question_text:
Longest Arithmetic Subsequence

Given an array nums of integers, return the length of the longest arithmetic subsequence in nums.

A subsequence is a subset of elements from an array written in the same order as the original array. An arithmetic sequence has a common difference between consecutive elements.

Constraints:

  • 2 <= nums.length <= 1000
  • 0 <= nums[i] <= 500
output_format: The length of the longest arithmetic subsequence.
explanation:
Dynamic Programming with Hash Map

For each position i, maintain a dictionary dp[i] mapping difference d → length of longest arithmetic subsequence ending at i with common difference d. For each pair (j, i) where j < i, compute d = nums[i] - nums[j] and set dp[i][d] = dp[j].get(d, 1) + 1. Track the global maximum.

Time: O(N²). Space: O(N²).
